author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
śatakoṭī gopī mādhava⋅mana
rākhite nārila kari’ jatana

veṇugīte ḍāke rādhikā⋅nāma
‘esa esa rādhe!’ ḍākaye śyāma

bhāṅgiyā śrī⋅rāsa⋅ maṇḍala tabe
rādhā⋅anveṣaṇe calaye jabe

‘dekhā diyā rādhe! rākhaha prāṇa!’
baliyā kā̐daye kānane kāna

nirjana⋅kānane rādhāre dhari’
miliyā parāṇa juḍāya hari

bale, ‘tu̐hu vinā kāhāra rāsa ?
tu̐hu lāgi’ mora varaja vāsa’

ehena rādhikā⋅ caraṇa⋅tale
bhakativinoda kā̐diyā bale

“tuyā gaṇa⋅mājhe āmāre gaṇi’
kiṅkarī kariyā rākha āpani”
en_translation:
Hundreds of millions of Gopī, despite their efforts, could not hold Mādhava's heart.

With the song of the flute, Śyāma calls Rādhikā's name, "Come, come, Rādhe!"

Breaking the glorious rāsa dance circle, when He goes in search of Rādhā,

Saying, "Show Yourself, Rādhe! Save My life!", Kṛṣṇa cries in the forest.

In the secluded forest, finding Rādhā, Hari's heart becomes peaceful upon their union.

He says, "Without You, whose rāsa dance is it? For Your sake is My residence in Vraja."

At the feet of such a Rādhikā, Bhakativinoda says, crying:

"Please consider me among Your associates and keep me as Your maidservant."
